title: Derive did:web from actor URL
description:
Compute did:web from actor URL. did:web is resolvable via HTTPS, serves as "current home" pointer.

## Derivation
Actor URL: https://example.com/users/alice
did:web: did:web:example.com:users:alice

Rules (did:web spec):
- https:// → did:web:
- Path / → :
- Port if non-443: did:web:example.com%3A8080:users:alice

## Implementation
```python
from urllib.parse import urlparse, quote

def actor_url_to_did_web(actor_url: str) -> str:
    parsed = urlparse(actor_url)
    host = parsed.netloc
    # Encode port if present
    if : in host:
        host = host.replace(:, %3A)
    # Path without leading slash, colons for slashes
    path = parsed.path.lstrip(/).replace(/, :)
    return f"did:web:{host}:{path}"
```

## Notes
- did:web is computed, not stored (derivable from actor URL)
- If actor URL changes (migration), did:web changes (that is expected)
- did:key stays constant across migrations (that is the point)

## Acceptance
- [ ] did_web property on actor computes correctly
- [ ] Handles ports, paths, edge cases
- [ ] Round-trips: did:web resolves back to actor
